summary: Decompiled additional Yoshi's Story ground/stage behavior in gryorster, replacing stubs/UNK signatures with typed implementations for track element setup, collision/material callbacks, and per-frame track element state updates. The PR also refined Yorster-related parameter and GroundVars structs so the new code could address meaningful fields instead of padding.
what_changed: Added required includes in gryorster.c for grlib, grmaterial, it_26B1, lb_00B0, and baselib/jobj to support the newly decompiled code.
Expanded YorsterParams from mostly padding plus x14 into typed fields x00, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C, reflecting the parameter table used by the track logic.
Added pad_04[4] to grYt_804D6A20_t after its YorsterParams pointer, preserving global struct layout.

Implemented grYorster_802022A4, initializing nine Yorster track elements for joints 0xA through 0x12, creating material objects for each, registering mpJointSetCb2 collision callbacks, configuring materials, and starting ground animation 7.
Extended grYorster_80202428 so material interaction accumulates value into the matching track element's x04 field while still applying ftLib_80086A4C to fighters.
Replaced the grYorster_802024F0 UNK stub with a typed collision callback taking Ground, joint_id, CollData, unused, mpLib_GroundEnum, and f32 value parameters.

Implemented grYorster_802024F0 collision handling for environment kinds 1, 2, and 3, using ftLib and item knockback/velocity helpers when collision values exceed YorsterParams thresholds, and accumulating per-joint values into track element x08.

Implemented grYorster_8020266C as the per-frame state machine over nine track elements, coordinating material activation, animation frame selection, mp joint list updates/removals, translation checks, and reset behavior.
Updated gryorster.h to include mp/forward.h and replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for grYorster_802024F0 with a concrete prototype.
Expanded struct grYorster_TrackElement in gr/types.h from padding plus x4/x1C into named offset fields x00, x01, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C.
smart_moves: Used a fixed local joints array for the nine Yorster track elements, making the repeated joint setup explicit and keeping joint IDs tied to element indices.
Preserved offset-oriented field names such as x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C where semantic names were not yet evidenced, avoiding overclaiming while still eliminating opaque padding.
Converted the collision callback from an unknown signature to a typed signature only after adding the required forward declaration for mpLib_GroundEnum.
Matched the state machine with explicit integer states in entry->x01 rather than prematurely inventing enum names, which keeps the decomp evidence-grounded.
Handled animation end-frame clamping through lbGetJObjEndFrame before computing frame advances, reflecting a careful reconstruction of runtime behavior.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in grYorster_802024F0, indicating an assembly-matching tactic for stack layout rather than purely source-level cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling stage-specific GroundVars, first recover the per-element struct layout enough to express loops and callbacks; this PR shows that replacing padding with offset fields can unlock several related functions at once.
Stage collision callback signatures can often be inferred from mpJointSetCb2 call sites and CollData usage; once typed, downstream code becomes much clearer.
For stage mechanics driven by map joints, keep map-space joint IDs and resolved mpLib joint IDs distinct: this PR stores the map joint in x14 and resolves it with Ground_801C32D4 when needed.
Material callback objects created with grMaterial_801C8CFC can carry per-element identity by comparing the callback's item_gobj against stored HSD_GObj pointers.
Do not force semantic names when the PR evidence only proves offsets and rough roles; x04/x08 accumulation fields and x01 state are useful stepping stones for later naming.

Per-frame stage update code may interleave animation, material, collision, and visibility/position checks; matching it often requires headers across grmaterial, grlib, lb, mp, ft, item, and baselib jobj.
naming_conventions: Stage symbols keep the address-based grYorster_8020XXXX naming style.
Data symbols use the existing grYt_803E5198, grYt_803E51CC, and grYt_804D6A20 naming convention.

Newly exposed struct fields retain offset-style names where semantics are not proven, such as YorsterParams.x00/x04/x08/x0C/x10/x14/x18/x1C and grYorster_TrackElement.x00/x01/x04/x08/x0C/x10/x14/x18/x1C.
The per-element type is named struct grYorster_TrackElement, aligning the type with the stage module and its apparent track-element role.
Function prototypes use project typedefs and forward-declared engine types such as Ground, CollData, mpLib_GroundEnum, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, Vec3, DynamicsDesc, and enum_t.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in grYorster_802024F0 to satisfy stack matching constraints.
Kept odd expression form ftLib_800873F4((0, coll_data->x0_gobj)), which likely preserves a compiler/codegen quirk needed for matching.
Used explicit casts to f32 and s32 around parameter fields and computed frame counts to mirror expected code generation.
Used switch cases on (s8) entry->x01, preserving signed-byte state behavior.

Retained numeric constants such as joints 0xA-0x12, animation id 7, states 1-4, frame thresholds 0x143 and 0x19, and scale factors 10.0f, 5.0f, 1.0f, 0.3f, and 0.5f rather than inventing unsupported constants.
Called lbGetJObjEndFrame twice in the clamping branches rather than caching it, matching the source pattern shown in the diff.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR context, and the human text excerpt is empty; no reviewer guidance can be extracted from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename grYorster_TrackElement fields x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, or x1C to semantic names.
Search for other stage modules using grMaterial_801C8CFC and mpJointSetCb2 together as a pattern for decompiling material-triggered collision mechanics.
Compare grYorster_8020266C against matching assembly or later cleanup to confirm the meaning of states 1, 2, 3, and 4 in entry->x01.
Search for documentation or later struct updates for YorsterParams to identify what x00, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x18, and x1C represent.
